item.name: Products
item.description:
Manage products, product models, and their media files.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#Products

item.name: Product [uuid]
item.description:
Manage products using UUID identifiers (recommended). UUIDs are system-generated unique identifiers that remain stable across environments.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#products-uuid

request.name: Create a new product
request.description:
Create a new product using UUID identifiers.

If no `uuid` is provided, the PIM generates one automatically. The product is created in the draft state if your user permissions require approval.

**Required fields**: Only `family` is strongly recommended. All other fields are optional.

**Attribute values**: Follow the locale/scope structure — set `locale: null` for non-localizable attributes and `scope: null` for non-scopable attributes.

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

originalRequest.name: Create a new product
originalRequest.description:
Create a new product using UUID identifiers.

If no `uuid` is provided, the PIM generates one automatically. The product is created in the draft state if your user permissions require approval.

**Required fields**: Only `family` is strongly recommended. All other fields are optional.

**Attribute values**: Follow the locale/scope structure — set `locale: null` for non-localizable attributes and `scope: null` for non-scopable attributes.

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Delete a product
request.description:
Permanently delete a product by its UUID. This action cannot be undone.

The product is removed from all categories and associations. If the product has variants, you must delete those first.

originalRequest.name: Delete a product

request.name: Get a draft
request.description:
Retrieve the current draft of a product — the working copy with unapproved changes (Enterprise Edition).

The draft contains changes that haven't been approved yet. Compare with the published product to see what's pending.

response.name: Draft details

exec:

});
// Validate if response has JSON Body 
pm.test("[GET]::/api/rest/v1/products-uuid/:uuid/draft - Response has JSON Body", function () {
    pm.response.to.have.jsonBody();

request.name: Get a product
request.description:
Retrieve a single product by its UUID.

Optionally include quality scores, completeness data, and attribute option labels using query parameters.

**Tip**: Set `with_attribute_options=true` to get human-readable labels for select attributes instead of just option codes.

description.content: Return labels of attribute options in the response.
Return asset collection share link URLs in the response.
Return product quality scores in the response.
Return product completenesses information in the response.
Return the root parent product model code of a variant in the response.
Return an array of workflowexecutionstatuses directly associated with the requested entity (product or product model) in the response.

response.name: Product details

request.name: Get list of products
request.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of products using UUID identifiers.

Products can be filtered by category, family, completeness, quality score, attributes, and more using the `search` query parameter. Enable disabled search filters one at a time to try different filter combinations.

**Pagination**: Use `page` type (default, max 10,000 products) or `search_after` type (cursor-based, unlimited). For large catalogs, always use `search_after`.

**Tip**: Use `attributes` to limit which attribute values are returned — this significantly improves response time for products with many attributes.

Specify which page of results to retrieve when using pagination.

Filter product values to return scopable attributes for the given channel as well as the non localizable/non scopable attributes.

Filter the products by their family
Filter the products by their creation date
Filter the products updated since X days
Filter the variant products by their parent

Get all variant products
Get all simple products
Filter the products with a "D" for the ecommerce channel and en_US locale
Filter the products with an "A" or "B" for the mobile channel and en_GB locale
Get only enabled products
Get only enabled products that are at least 70% complete on the ecommerce channel
Filter the products by an attribute value
Filter the products having a description begining with Amazing on the en_US locale
Filter the products that have a release date due after the 4th of July 2016 for the ecommerce channel
Filter the products containing a text in the name attribute
Filter the products by multiple attribute values
Filter the products by multiple attribute conditions
Cursor when using the search_after pagination method type.
Filter locales based on specific criteria.
Filter scopes based on specific criteria.
Return asset collection share link URLs in the response.
Return labels of attribute options in the response.
Return product completenesses information in the response.
Return the count of items in the response.
Return product quality scores in the response.
Return the root parent product model code of a variant in the response.
Return an array of workflowexecutionstatuses directly associated with the requested entity (product or product model) in the response.

request.description:
Search for products using filters provided in the request body (POST).

This endpoint is functionally equivalent to GET with `search` query parameters, but allows complex filter expressions in the body instead of URL-encoded parameters. Useful when filters would make the URL too long.

**Tip**: Combine multiple filters to narrow results — e.g. enabled + completeness + category.

request.name: Submit a draft for approval
request.description:
Submit a product draft for approval in the workflow (Enterprise Edition).

The product must have pending changes in draft state. Once submitted, the draft enters the approval workflow and the assigned reviewer is notified.

description.content: (Required)

request.name: Update/create a product
request.description:
Update an existing product or create it if the UUID doesn't exist yet.

**Update behavior**: Uses merge-patch semantics. Only include the fields you want to change.
- To update a single attribute value, send only that attribute in `values`
- To clear a value, set `data` to `null`
- Arrays (categories, groups) are replaced entirely, not merged

See: https://api.akeneo.com/documentation/update.html

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Update/create several products
request.description:
Batch update and/or create multiple products in a single request.

Send one JSON object per line (NDJSON format) with `Content-Type: application/vnd.akeneo.collection+json`. Each line is processed independently — if one fails, the others still succeed.

**Update behavior**: Uses merge-patch semantics. Only the fields you send are modified. To clear a value, explicitly set it to `null`.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/documentation/update.html

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

item.name: Product [identifier]
item.description:
Manage products using the legacy SKU/identifier system. For new integrations, prefer the UUID endpoints.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#products

request.name: Create a new product
request.description:
Create a new product using identifier (SKU).

The `identifier` field is required and must be unique. For new integrations, prefer UUID endpoints.

Attribute values follow the locale/scope structure: `locale: null` for non-localizable, `scope: null` for non-scopable.

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Delete a product
request.description: Permanently delete a product by identifier. Cannot be undone.

request.name: Get a draft
request.description: Retrieve the current draft of a product by identifier (Enterprise Edition).

request.name: Get list of products
request.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of products using legacy identifiers (SKU).

For new integrations, prefer the UUID endpoint. Same filters as UUID: category, family, completeness, quality score, attributes, etc.

Pagination: Use `page` (default, max 10,000) or `search_after` (cursor-based, unlimited).

Tip: Use `attributes` to limit returned values for better performance.

request.name: Submit a draft for approval
request.description: Submit a product draft for approval (Enterprise Edition).

request.name: Update/create a product
request.description:
Update a product by identifier or create it if it does not exist.

Merge-patch semantics: only send fields you want to change. Arrays (categories, groups) are replaced entirely.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/documentation/update.html

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Update/create several products
request.description:
Batch update/create products by identifier. One JSON per line (NDJSON).

Each line is processed independently. Content-Type must be `application/vnd.akeneo.collection+json`.

Update behavior: merge-patch. Only fields you send are modified.

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

response.name: Batch results

item.name: Product model
item.description:
Manage product models — the parent structures for products with variants (e.g. a T-shirt model with size/color variants).

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#product-models

request.name: Create a new product model
request.description:
Create a new product model.

**Required**: `code`, `family_variant`
**Optional**: `parent` (for sub-models), `categories`, `values`

**Hierarchy example**:
- Root model: `model-tshirt` (no parent) — holds shared values like brand, name
- Sub-model: `model-tshirt-blue` (parent: model-tshirt) — holds color-level values
- Variant product: created separately, references the sub-model

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Delete a product model
request.description:
Delete a product model and ALL its children (sub-models and variant products).

**Warning**: This is destructive. All variant products under this model will also be deleted. Cannot be undone.

request.description: Retrieve the current draft of a product model (Enterprise Edition).

request.name: Get list of product models
request.description:
List product models with pagination and filters.

Filters work similarly to product filters:
- `categories` — IN, NOT IN
- `family` — IN, NOT IN
- `completeness` — ALL COMPLETE, AT LEAST COMPLETE, etc.
- `parent` — IN, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL
- `created` / `updated` — date comparisons

**Tip**: Use `parent IS NULL` to get only root-level models.

description.content: Filter product values to only return those concerning the given attributes.
Convert values of measurement attributes to the unit configured in the channel provided by the scope parameter.
Specify the maximum number of results to return per page.
Filter product values to return localizable attributes for the given locales as well as the non localizable/non scopable attributes.
Specify which page of results to retrieve when using pagination.
Specify which pagination method to use for navigating through large datasets.
Filter product values to return scopable attributes for the given channel as well as the non localizable/non scopable attributes.

Filter product models,
for more details see the [Filters](https://api.
Filter the product models by their completeness
Filter the product models that have at least one variant product 100% complete
Filter the product models that are in the camcorders family,
Filter the product models that are not in the camcorders and digital_cameras family
Filter the product models by their creation date
Filter the product models updated since X days
Filter the sub-product models of the root product model with the code tshirt_armor
Filter the root product models (product models without parent)
Filter the sub-product models (product models with parent)
Cursor when using the search_after pagination method type.
Filter locales based on specific criteria.
Filter scopes based on specific criteria.
Return asset collection share link URLs in the response.
Return labels of attribute options in the response.
Return the count of items in the response.
Return product model quality scores in the response.
Return an array of workflowexecutionstatuses directly associated with the requested entity (product or product model) in the response.

request.description: Submit a product model draft for approval (Enterprise Edition).

request.name: Update/create a product model
request.description:
Update or create a product model.

**Immutable fields**: `family_variant` cannot be changed after creation.
**Update behavior**: Merge-patch — only send the fields you want to change.

description.content: When present in the query string (regardless of value), automatically creates missing attribute options during product/product model creation or update.

request.name: Update/create several product models
request.description:
Batch update/create product models. One JSON per line (NDJSON).

**Tip**: When creating a hierarchy, send root models first, then sub-models.

item.name: Product media file
item.description:
Upload, retrieve, and download media files (images, PDFs, etc.) attached to product attribute values.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#media-files

item.name: Create a new product media file

request.name: Create a new product media file
request.description:
Upload a new media file and attach it to a product or product model attribute.

Send as `multipart/form-data` with:
- `file` — the binary file to upload
- `product` — JSON: `{"identifier": "my_product", "attribute": "picture", "locale": "en_US", "scope": "ecommerce"}`

For UUID products, use `uuid` instead of `identifier` in the product JSON.

**Max file size**: Depends on your PIM configuration (default 10MB).

Manage categories — the tree structure used to classify products. Categories can be enriched with additional attributes.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#categories

request.name: Create a category media file
request.description:
Upload a media file (image) for an enriched category attribute.

Send as `multipart/form-data` with:
- `file` — the binary file
- `product` — JSON object: `{"code": "my_category", "attribute": "image_attr", "locale": "en_US", "channel": "ecommerce"}`

description.content: (Required) Equal to 'multipart/form-data' (with [boundary value](https://www.w3.org/TR/html401/interact/forms.html#h-17.13.4.2)), no other value allowed.

(Required) The category to which the media file will be associated.
It is a JSON string that follows this format:
'{"code":"category code", "attribute_code":"attribute code", "channel":"channel code or null", "locale":"locale code or null"}'.

(Required) The media file to be uploaded.
It should be provided as a binary file.
Ensure that the file format is supported by the system.

request.name: Create a new category
request.description:
Create a new category.

- Set `parent: null` to create a root category (category tree)
- Set `parent: "code"` to nest under an existing category
- `values` supports enriched attributes (text, images, booleans) per channel/locale
- `validations` controls product assignment rules

request.name: Create or update a category

Update or create a category by code.

**Warning**: Moving a category (changing `parent`) moves all its children too. This operation can be slow for large sub-trees.

request.name: Create or update several categories
request.description:
Batch create/update categories. One JSON per line (NDJSON).

**Tip**: When creating a tree, send the parent category before its children to avoid errors.

request.name: Download a category media file
request.description:
Download a media file associated with an enriched category.

The `{{categoryMediaFilePath}}` is the path returned when the file was uploaded or in the category values.

Retrieve a single category by code.

**Options**:
- `with_position=true` — includes the category's position within its parent
- `with_enriched_attributes=true` — includes enriched category attribute values (images, descriptions, etc.)

- Find by codes: `code IN ["code1", "code2"]`
- Recently updated: `updated > "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"`

Obtain an OAuth2 access token using the password grant type.

**Setup**: Provide your Connection's `clientId` and `secret` as collection variables. The pre-request script automatically handles Basic authentication.

**Token lifetime**: Access tokens expire after 1 hour. Use the `refresh_token` to get a new access token without re-authenticating.

**App tokens**: If you're using a Connected App, set the `app_access_token` environment variable instead — the pre-request script will use it directly.

item.name: Channel
item.description:
Manage channels (scopes) — represent distribution channels like ecommerce, mobile, print. Each channel has its own locales, currencies, and completeness requirements.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#channels

request.name: Create a new channel
request.description:
Create a new channel.

**Required fields**: `code`, `category_tree`, `currencies` (at least one), `locales` (at least one).

**Tip**: `conversion_units` lets you auto-convert metrics — e.g. display weight in KILOGRAM on ecommerce but POUND on a US channel.

request.name: Get a channel
request.description:
Retrieve a single channel (scope) by code. Channels represent distribution contexts like ecommerce, mobile, or print.

Returns the channel's locales, currencies, category tree, and conversion units.

request.name: Get a list of channels
request.description:
List all channels. Channels define which locales, currencies, and category tree are used for each distribution context.

Channels also determine completeness requirements — a product is 'complete' when all required attributes for that channel are filled.

description.content: Specify which page of results to retrieve when using pagination.

// Validate if response header has matching content-type
pm.test("[GET]::/api/rest/v1/catalogs/:id/products/:uuid - Content-Type is application/json", function () {
   pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get("Content-Type")).to.include("application/json");
});
// Validate if response has JSON Body 
pm.test("[GET]::/api/rest/v1/catalogs/:id/products/:uuid - Response has JSON Body", function () {
    pm.response.to.have.jsonBody();
});

item.name: Mapping schema for products
item.description:
Define how PIM product data maps to your application's data model for a catalog.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/apps/catalogs.html

item.name: Create or update the product mapping schema related to a catalog

request.description:
Define or replace the mapping schema for a catalog.

The schema maps Akeneo attributes to your app's fields.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/apps/catalogs.html#step-1-define-your-json-schema

item.name: Delete the product mapping schema related to a catalog

request.name: Delete the product mapping schema related to a catalog
request.description: Remove the mapping schema. Mapped product endpoints will return 404 until a new schema is configured.

item.name: Get the product mapping schema related to a catalog

request.name: Get the product mapping schema related to a catalog
request.description:
Retrieve the JSON Schema defining how PIM data maps to your app's model.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/apps/catalogs.html

item.description:
Manage workflows, workflow executions, and workflow tasks.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#Workflows

item.name: Workflows
item.description:
Manage workflows — automated approval processes for product enrichment (Enterprise Edition only).

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#workflows

request.name: Get a workflow
request.description: Retrieve a workflow with its steps and configuration.

item.name: Get list of workflow step assignees

request.name: Get list of workflow step assignees
request.description: List users assigned to a workflow step — the enrichers, reviewers, or approvers responsible for tasks at that step.

description.content: Specify which page of results to retrieve when using pagination.
Specify the maximum number of results to return per page.

request.name: Get list of workflows
request.description:
List all workflows (Enterprise Edition).

Workflows define multi-step approval processes: Draft -> Review -> Approve -> Publish.

description.content: Specify which page of results to retrieve when using pagination.
Specify the maximum number of results to return per page.

item.name: Workflow executions
item.description:
Start workflow executions on products or product models.

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#workflow-executions

request.name: Start several workflow executions
request.description:
Start workflow executions on products/product models.

Each item pairs a workflow UUID with either `product.uuid` or `product_model.code`.

Use case: Trigger approval workflows after a bulk import.

item.name: Workflow tasks
item.description:
List and update workflow tasks (approve, reject, complete).

See: https://api.akeneo.com/api-reference-index.html#workflow-tasks

request.name: Get list of workflow tasks
request.description:
List workflow tasks — work items assigned to users.

Filter by workflow, step, assignee, or status.

Task types: `enrichment` (fill data), `review` (approve/reject).

item.name: Update a Workflow Task's Status (Approve, Reject, Complete)

request.name: Update a Workflow Task's Status (Approve, Reject, Complete)
request.description:
Complete, approve, or reject a task.

Enrichment step: `status: "completed"`
Review step: `status: "approved"` or `status: "rejected"`

Rejection requires `send_back_to_step_uuid` and `rejected_attributes` with comments per attribute/locale/scope.

info.name: Akeneo PIM REST API
info.description:
Complete Akeneo PIM REST API collection with realistic examples for every endpoint.

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` to your PIM URL (e.g. `https://my-pim.cloud.akeneo.com`)
2. Set `clientId` and `secret` from your PIM Connection or App
3. Set `username` and `password` (or set `app_access_token` for App tokens)

Authentication is handled automatically via the pre-request script.
